source.title: Documented seed: cover with orbit/core concept
source.source_note: This extraction is reconstructed from the documented OpenClaw style lessons and validated slide patterns, not from the original slide screenshot.

claim: Frame the deck as a system transition with a strong technology anchor rather than a title-only opener.
visual_anchor: A dominant orbit or core object on the right side creates the premium focal point.

composition.structure: Large left title block paired with a large right-side system object and a compact metadata band.
composition.alignment_logic: All text blocks share one left grid; the orbit object is contained within a right-side frame with stable margins.
composition.asymmetry: The slide is intentionally right-heavy because the hero object carries the visual weight.
composition.image_usage: Hero visual or SVG object is required to prevent a plain title page.
highlight_grammar: high-contrast headline with limited accent color
glow or gradient effects only around the hero object
small signal labels instead of scattered decorative text

components.position_note: Compact metadata or concept labels near the text grid

why_it_works: The page establishes a premium focal point before the reader scans the title.
Asymmetry creates energy without breaking alignment discipline.
The hero object makes the cover feel like a strategic system page, not a generic template.
anti_patterns: Reducing the hero object to a small icon makes the slide lose impact.
Adding multiple equal cards turns the cover into an agenda page.
Letting labels drift away from the headline grid weakens the composition.

adaptation_notes: Keep the one-large-object principle even if the visual changes from orbit to product or architecture motif.
Preserve the left text grid and right hero balance.
